title: Jessie Buckley Wins Golden Globe for Best Actress Drama - Variety
text: Jessie Buckley earned the Golden Globe for Best Actress in a Drama at the 83rd Golden Globes ceremony on January 11, 2026, for her role in Hamnet. This victory follows Buckley's recent Critics Choice Award win and Screen Actors Guild nomination. She competed against Jennifer Lawrence, Renate Reinsve, Julia Roberts, Tessa Thompson, and Eva Victor. The award marked her first Golden Globe nomination. Buckley opened by saying: This is not a normal feeling or situation to be in, but thank you, Golden Globes, thank you Focus and everybody who supported this film. She thanked the film's key grip, Tomasz Sternicki, describing a touching moment. The Oscar-winning director Chloé Zhao's film stars Buckley as Agnes Shakespeare alongside Paul Mescal. Hamnet earned six Golden Globes nominations total, winning both Best Drama and Buckley's award.
